title: High-key-rate Fully-Passive Quantum Access Network with Thermal Source
authors: H. W. Yin, B. D. Zhu, H. Peng, T. Wang, X. Q. Jiang, Y. K. Xu, G. H. Zeng
abstract: To accommodate classical communication systems with progressively increasing transmission rates, quantum access networks (QAN) have undergone systematic and protocol-level optimizations in recent years, where quantum passive optical network (QPON) architectures are gaining significant attention due to their simple structure. It is challenging for the previous QAN based on active protocols or Stokes operator coding protocols to achieve high-speed linear modulation with high extinction ratio and stability under practical conditions. In this work, we propose and experimentally demonstrate a downstream fully passive quantum access network protocol using passive state preparation (PSP) with free-space and single-mode fiber hybrid channels, and the final key generation rate is up to a record-breaking 19.48 Mbps per quantum network unit. The proposed PSP-QPON scheme extends the scope of PSP-CVQKD from point-to-point to point-to-multi-point networks, which enables high-key-rate, high-stability, and low-resource-consumption implementation. Moreover, the network channel in this experiment is fully compatible with access networks in classical optical communications, which allows integration with existing optical infrastructure without the need for additional modifications, providing a promising solution for local area network quantum access network at home or a mobile terminal.
summary: The paper presents an experimental demonstration of a fully passive quantum access network that achieves a record-breaking key rate of 19.48 Mbps. By using a passive state preparation scheme, the researchers show a scalable point-to-multi-point architecture that is compatible with existing classical optical fiber infrastructure.
main_problem: Achieving high-speed, stable, and low-resource quantum access networks (QAN) that can scale from point-to-point to point-to-multi-point without the instability of active protocols.
main_result: Demonstration of a downstream fully passive quantum access network protocol achieving a record-breaking key generation rate of 19.48 Mbps per quantum network unit.
method: The researchers implemented a passive state preparation (PSP) scheme within a quantum passive optical network (QPON) architecture using hybrid free-space and single-mode fiber channels.
model_or_system: A downstream quantum access network utilizing a passive state preparation (PSP) protocol for continuous-variable quantum key distribution (CVQKD) across a point-to-multi-point architecture.
key_observables: Final key generation rate (Mbps per quantum network unit).
important_parameters: Key generation rate of 19.48 Mbps; hybrid free-space and single-mode fiber channels.
main_assumptions: The network is compatible with existing classical optical communication infrastructure.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the motivation for optimized QANs, proposes a passive state preparation (PSP) protocol, demonstrates experimental implementation using hybrid channels, and discusses compatibility with classical infrastructure.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant to quantum optics and quantum information as it demonstrates a scalable, high-rate architecture for quantum communication networks using passive components.
